# Agent Mail

> Create disposable, programmable email inboxes on the fly — for humans and AI agents.
> Send mail to `anything@<key>.<domain>` and it instantly appears in the space tied to
> that key. An MCP server lets AI agents provision a mailbox and read mail (OTPs,
> confirmations, magic links) during automated workflows. Self-hostable (MIT) or managed.

Agent Mail does not implement SMTP/IMAP itself: a standard mail server (Postfix)
receives mail into a Maildir, and the backend polls it, routes by key/verified-domain,
and serves a REST API consumed by the web UI and the MCP server. An instance is a
private space identified by a secret UUID (the only credential).

## For AI agents

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an agent provisions a mailbox and reads mail; conventions.
- [MCP server](docs/mcp.md): tools (create_mailbox, list_emails, get_email,
  wait_for_email, add/verify custom domains) and agent recipes.
- [Examples](examples/README.md): runnable MCP config, Python OTP flow, and curl recipes.

## Documentation

- [API reference](docs/api.md): every REST endpoint with request/response shapes.
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): components, data model, recipient routing.
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md): run locally, self-host with DNS + Postfix.
- [Security](docs/security.md): trust model and hardening checklist.

## Key facts

- Address shape: `anything@<key>.<EMAIL_DOMAIN>` routes by the single subdomain label
  `<key>`. A bare custom domain routes once its MX is verified.
- Auth: the instance secret UUID is a bearer credential in the request path; an optional
  `X-Admin-Token` gates admin/mutating endpoints when configured.
- `body_html` in stored mail is attacker-controlled; prefer `body_text`.
